title: NQBlade Performance
author: Some_Fly_4552
selftext:
Hello we recently made a new update of our Trading Bot if you’d like to check it out just give me a quick dm✌️

General Info: 

NQBlade is a rule-based NAS100 trading bot built around a trend-following approach. Instead of taking random entries, it waits for multiple conditions to line up, including trend direction, higher-timeframe confirmation, volatility filters, and structured risk management.

Each setup is managed in a clear and disciplined way with stop loss protection, partial take profits, and a trailing stop, aiming to control downside while still allowing larger moves to develop.

The overall goal is not to overtrade, but to focus on cleaner setups, controlled execution, and consistent trade management.

comments.author: EngineeringLoose2320
comments.body: Can you share any configuration details? GitHub source? Would love to try to implement something similar

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: Give me a quick dm

comments.author: Practical_Estate4971
comments.body: Let me guess this amazing bot costs hundreds of dollars despite you showing how it can print money.

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: It does not, if you want more info just contact me

replies.author: OneDropOfOcean
replies.body:
It's going to bullshit. It's always Bullshit. 

Sign up to this, pay me that, but message me first!

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: Ah yeah? Just see some broker statements I posted recently is that bullshit too?

replies.author: Practical_Estate4971
replies.body: Most likely. Why would you spend time selling this if it makes you money?

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: Bc why not build a business around sth that works?

replies.author: LarsVG18
replies.body: Your bot makes 40% per 4 months, just keep it running for 5 years and you have 1.5 million, no need to sell it. In fact you'll be a billionaire in just over 10 years. We both know you won't be, just another AI slop bot.

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: There are backtest from 5 years just look at my channel before commenting BS🤷‍♂️✌️

replies.author: LarsVG18
replies.body: You mean the last 5 bull years conveniently right after covid? I have a one line algorithm that beats yours: buy leveraged s&p. Done.

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: Fair point, but the last 5 years still weren’t just a straight line up. There were plenty of sharp selloffs, volatility spikes, and messy range phases in between. So yes, bullish index bias helped, but it’s still more than just “buy leveraged S&P and sit there.”

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: First you should try it out and then make a judgement before commenting sth like that🤷‍♂️

replies.author: OneDropOfOcean
replies.body:
OK, can I try it out. But i don't want to sign up to your telegram, nor pay you any money. 

How does that sound?

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: You don’t need to you can try it out and test it ofc

replies.author: OneDropOfOcean
replies.body: ok cool. How do I do that?

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: DM me and we’ll go forward🤷‍♂️

comments.author: TexasGater
comments.body: i just don't understand the greed on these bots. if they work so well just release them or their GitHub to the masses and share the wealth. why is it always about secrecy. i mean most of these bots are vibe coded now anyway so there is no to very little financial cost to the person that made it.

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: Fair point, but cheap to build doesn’t mean worthless. The real value usually isn’t just the code, it’s the time spent testing, refining, and making it work in live markets. That’s why most people don’t just throw it on GitHub for free.

comments.body: Dude has literally posted this on all possible channels 😂

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: Why not?

comments.author: detoxed696
comments.body: I’m interested can you send me more info

comments.author: Uoju
comments.body:
Watching these people find new ways to rub two pennys together and sell it to yall is almost hilarious. The guy made a difference trading bot and is hoping whoever buys in doesn’t do the math on volume trades or the average win rates. 

Anyways L-O-L and remember no crying at the casino

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: Fair enough if that’s your view, but it’s a bit more nuanced than that. A bot can still have positive expectancy even with moderate win rates if the trade management and RR make sense. I’m also not pretending it’s risk-free or some holy grail, every system has weak phases, drawdowns, and limits. If someone isn’t into it, that’s completely fine, but calling everything “casino” without looking at the actual structure is just lazy.

replies.author: Uoju
replies.body:
This bot, like the rest, are the new vehicles to get bag holders in. I will commend you on being early to the idea and hope you have a higher tier of bot to farm the lower ones. 
Also the US stock market is a full on casino but with less fan fair.

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: Fair enough, but I’m not acting like it’s risk-free or pretending markets aren’t messy. Every system has drawdowns and weak phases. The difference is whether there’s actual structure, risk control, and positive expectancy behind it or not. If someone thinks it’s trash, that’s fine, but I’d still separate a rule-based system from just calling everything a scam.

comments.author: achilles6196
comments.body: If the bot actually works, why sell it instead of using it yourself? That's the real question.

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: Fair question. The simple answer is: I do use it myself. But even a profitable bot has drawdowns, risk, and scaling limits. Building a business around something you already use isn’t mutually exclusive. It’s just diversifying income instead of relying on a single strategy forever.

comments.author: Vana987r
comments.body:
I want to give OP the benefit of the doubt, we all are training our trading bot. But the Idea to give OP money is out of the question, Dm OP is out the question, OP 's telegram is out of the question..

OP seems legit and if it was a scam I would not believe it. But I think OP is dreaming about getting rich through (dm me on reddit).

OP created a bot, OP can create a website and advertise for his product on the internet, OP would probably have a better financial return on "time invested" because this is what OP is selling, his "time invested" tuning his beautiful trading bot that will make him a billionaire in 10 years, hell you can even retire after one year.

So wait second, on second thoughts this seems to be just like another scam, May be OP does not even know he is scamming people's time.

Sorry OP, My dad is Skepticism and my mum is Frowning at you.

comments.author: Kay_ien
comments.body:
Pues, no se los demás, pero yo personalmente se de sobra el tiempo y rompeduras de cabeza que conlleva creae un maldito bot que funcione, especialmente cuando estás comenzando.

Paso mucho tiempo en reddit leyendo, buscando consejos y orientaciones, aunque no me atrevo a postear porque, depende del post, te comen.

Yo entiendo que lo vendas, cualquier persona a la que le gusten las inversiones sigue una de las normas básicas: diversifica xD

Aunque estaría bien ver de vez en cuando gente que aporte un poco de ayuda a los que aun estamos en el barro, tratando de aprender y dandonos de cabezazos sin tener que preguntar especificamente.

La explicacion de la construccion de algun bot rentable, aunque sea un turtle, consejos, donde no estancarte o encabezonarte... Nose, cosas así.

La comunidad de reddit me hace gracia, porque o son un puto amor, o son venenosos a más no poder xD

Enfin, buen trabajo, bro
